sections.paragraph:
εἶτα adv. (Soph., Thu.+; loanw. in rabb.).

① pert. to being next in order of time, then, next (placed first) εἶτα γενομένης θλίψεως then when oppression comes Mk 4:17; cp. 8:25; Lk 8:12; J 2:3 v.l.; 11:7 v.l.; 13:5; 19:27; 20:27; Js 1:15; 1 Cl 25:3; 56:13; B 8:2; 12:2; 13:2; Hv 3, 7, 3 al.; GPt 11:47. In enumerations: πρῶτον … εἶτα (X., An. 1, 3, 2; Philo Mech. 71, 26f; Epict. 1, 15, 7; 1, 26, 3 al.; BGU 665, 10 [I a.d.]; EpArist 77; Jos., Ant. 15, 149; Just., D. 8, 4 al.) first … then 1 Ti 2:13; 3:10; 1 Cor 12:28 v.l.; 1 Cl 23:4; 2 Cl 11:3; B 6:17. ἔπειτα … εἶτα after that … then (Galen XIII 740 K.) 1 Cor 15:7, 24; also εἶτα … ἔπειτα (Galen XIII 743 K.) vs. 5f. Since in enumerations εἶ. oft. serves to put things in juxtaposition without reference to chronological sequence, it becomes in general

② a transition word to mark an addition to someth. just stated, furthermore, then, next (Wsd 14:22; Ar. 10:3; Tat. 4:2) B 6:3; 11:10; 13:2; Dg 11:6, introducing a new argument in a demonstration Hb 12:9.—The Ionic-Hellenistic form εἶτεν (Phryn. 124 Lob.; SIG 972, 150 [175–172 b.c.]; 736, 30f [92 b.c.]; PGM 13, 400; Mayser 14; s. B-D-F §35, 3; Rob. 119, 160; M-M εἶτεν) is found as v.l. Mk 4:28.—DELG. M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Philo Mech Philo Mech , III–II b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

oft. oft. = often

someth. someth. = something

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Phryn Phryn , the Atticist, signifying his Eclogae, II a.d.—List 5

Lob. Lob. = CLobeck, s. Phryn.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Mayser Mayser = EM., Grammatik der griechischen Papyri aus der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 295
